question: What is done in the Organize part of the Architecture Design Process?

options.label: A
options.text: Concepts are disclosed to the Architecture team

options.label: B
options.text: Concepts are added to the layers of the Architecture Canvas

options.label: C
options.text: Concepts are assembled into Modules

correctText: Concepts are added to the layers of the Architecture Canvas

question: Which of the following is a benefit of having good architecture?

options.label: A
options.text: Unmanageable dependencies

options.label: B
options.text: Slow-moving legacy systems

options.label: C
options.text: Manages complexity

options.label: D
options.text: Poor service abstraction

correctText: Manages complexity

question: Which of the following Data elements can we find in a Foundation layer Module?

options.label: A
options.text: CRUD actions for Entities

options.label: B
options.text: Entities to save UI states

options.label: C
options.text: Non-core Entities

options.label: D
options.text: Core Entities (exposed as read-only)

correctText: Non-core Entities

question: _API module is for

options.label: A
options.text: Logic to Synchronize data in CS's with an external system. Isolating this logic makes the CS completely system agnostic and it’s easier to decouple or replace the external system.

options.label: B
options.text: Reusable Core Services with public entities, actions, and blocks.

options.label: C
options.text: A BL becomes a Calculation Engine if it performs complex calculations, (e.g. an invoice calculation engine or an insurance simulator). Engines are usually subject to versions.

options.label: D
options.text: Technical wrapper to expose an API to External consumers, keeping core services system agnostic and supporting multiple versions of the API.

options.label: E
options.text: Isolated Business Logic (Actions) or Core Widgets (blocks), to manage complexity, composition or to have its own lifecycle.

correctText: Technical wrapper to expose an API to External consumers, keeping core services system agnostic and supporting multiple versions of the API.

question: Which of the below best matches this statement : "Needed if data is coming from MULTIPLE external systems. IS will decide which driver to use depending on the data."

options.label: A
options.text: ECS with direct integration

options.label: B
options.text: ECS Summary cache only variation

options.label: C
options.text: Transparency Service Pattern 1 way (simplification)

options.label: D
options.text: ECS with publish/subscribe through an ESB

options.label: E
options.text: ECS Lazy Load variation

options.label: F
options.text: Base ECS pattern

options.label: G
options.text: Transparency Service Pattern

options.label: H
options.text: ECS with isolated synchronization logic

options.label: I
options.text: Library : Connector Pattern

options.label: J
options.text: Library : Extension Pattern

correctText: Transparency Service Pattern

question: Which of the following recommendations should be applied to the Core layer?

options.label: A
options.text: Core modules should not have business logic.

options.label: B
options.text: Core modules should have Front-end Screens for testing purposes.

options.label: C
options.text: Core modules should have public Read-only Entities.

options.label: D
options.text: Core modules should not have Core Entities.

correctText: Core modules should have public Read-only Entities.

question: What is NOT a best practice for Mobile Application Architecture: Limit server side calls?

options.label: A
options.text: No direct calls to backend systems. Do call to Outsystems Core Services and Outsystems will handle the interaction with external backend systems. Direct calls is bad from Security, Performance and Maintainability standpoint. Direct calls will require javascript Code and javascript Code is harder to maintain.

options.label: B
options.text: Synchronize on every screen or online event for better granularity

options.label: C
options.text: Limit Server side calls to online transactions or Synchronization. Do the bulk of the operations on the Client Side before passing information to the Server Side.

correctText: Synchronize on every screen or online event for better granularity

question: Which of the below are not recommendations for Architecture Validations

options.label: A
options.text: No front-end screens in Core Modules

options.label: B
options.text: No screens in the End User Layer

options.label: C
options.text: All public entities in Core Modules should be set to Read-only

options.label: D
options.text: No core entities in Foundational Modules

options.label: E
options.text: No business logic in the Foundational Modules

correctText: No screens in the End User Layer

question: "Splitting UI elements per functionality" is a best practice to avoid monolithic mobile UI modules? Which of the below is not a best practice?

options.label: A
options.text: Include the Common flow and Menu artifacts

options.label: B
options.text: Put CSS in blocks or screens. Allows better performance and seamless screen transitions

options.label: C
options.text: Screens as layouts that have placeholders and load content from blocks. The blocks come from CW modules.

options.label: D
options.text: CSS should only be placed in the mobile theme module

options.label: E
options.text: Blocks grouped in functional modules with independent life cycles

correctText: Put CSS in blocks or screens. Allows better performance and seamless screen transitions

question: Which of the below is NOT a reason for Validating a Modules' Architecture?

options.label: A
options.text: Optimizes lifecycle independence

options.label: B
options.text: Minimizes impact of changes

options.label: C
options.text: Stop unmanageable dependencies

options.label: D
options.text: Promotes microservices

options.label: E
options.text: Prevent slow deployments, inflexible legacy systems

options.label: F
options.text: Promotes abstraction of reusable services

options.label: G
options.text: Avoid poor service abstraction

correctText: Promotes microservices

question: Which of the following Data elements can we find in a End-User layer Module?

options.label: A
options.text: CRUD actions for Entities

options.label: B
options.text: Entities to save UI states

options.label: C
options.text: Core Entities (exposed as read-only)

options.label: D
options.text: Non-core Entities

correctText: Entities to save UI states

question: Which of the below is NOT a suitable advice for designing a LIGHTWEIGHT mobile local storage?

options.label: A
options.text: Define only used attributes

options.label: B
options.text: Denormalize the local storage

options.label: C
options.text: Store all records no matter if relevant or irrelevant

options.label: D
options.text: Group relevant information

correctText: Store all records no matter if relevant or irrelevant

question: What does NOT happen due to a lack of architecture concerns?

options.label: A
options.text: Poor service abstraction : Business concepts not correctly isolated, business rules tend to be spread over different systems and little to no code reuse

options.label: B
options.text: Inflexible and slow-moving legacy systems : adapting legacy systems to business changes may be difficult. Changes in complex and inflexible systems can take a long time

options.label: C
options.text: Tech Debt : AI Mentor will raise architectural tech debt such as cyclic dependency and side to side dependency

options.label: D
options.text: Unmanageable dependencies : System not isolated from each other. Updating or replacing a system has a cascade/snowball effect on other systems

correctText: Tech Debt : AI Mentor will raise architectural tech debt such as cyclic dependency and side to side dependency

question: Which of the below matches the most to Core Module Pattern - ECS with Publish/Subscribe through ESB...

options.label: A
options.text: ... a wrapper used to contain the logic, actions and data that will expose code that is inside of external library or to inspect external database and import the data structures so they can be used as entities inside of OS

options.label: B
options.text: Same as ECS with direct integration, but through an Enterprise Service Bus

options.label: C
options.text: Same as Base ECS pattern, but have a local replica. Store data to serve as a local cache. Pro: Leverage Entity Use, Simpler Integration API. Con: Less impact on source system

options.label: D
options.text: ... is a pattern with two modules, a connector module that can be used to encapsulate an external API with the input/output structures and a wrapper module to expose the normalized API to the consumers.

options.label: E
options.text: ... tries to fetch data from local cache entity, if not there, get single entry from the external system. Cache only that record (read-through caching) Use when whole database too big or costly to synchronize. Integration only touches a small portion of the database. Avoid if access to lists of data is needed up front

options.label: F
options.text: ... Entity is not in Outsystems but in an external ERP system. IS just makes remote call to external system/database. No data is being kept inside OS. Data retrieval may not be optimized as it needs to traverse two different systems to get the information back. Con: Integration API must support all use cases

options.label: G
options.text: ... Entity is exposed as read-only and API is available to centralize business logic for entity creation/update

options.label: H
options.text: Same as ECS with local replica but API module is provided. So any changes to the external system can notify OS, which OS then gets update from the ERP system (subscription system)

options.label: I
options.text: ... is needed if data is coming from MULTIPLE external systems. IS will decide which driver to use depending on the data.

options.label: J
options.text: Same as ECS with local replica but synchronization logic is separated. Pro: Code independence. Consumers of CS is not affected by Sync. Sync can orchestrate several CS

correctText: Same as ECS with direct integration, but through an Enterprise Service Bus

question: Which of the below is NOT a weak dependency as of OS11?

options.label: A
options.text: Service Actions

options.label: B
options.text: Server Actions

options.label: C
options.text: Screens

options.label: D
options.text: Local Storage Entities

options.label: E
options.text: Static Entities

options.label: F
options.text: Structures

options.label: G
options.text: Database Entities

correctText: Server Actions

question: OAPI is different compared to API. What is inside an OAPI?

options.label: A
options.text: REST API

options.label: B
options.text: Service Action

correctText: Service Action

question: In which of the following scenarios should you choose to clone a built-in Style Guide?

options.label: A
options.text: When it is not possible to benefit from any existing theme and extensive changes are needed.

options.label: B
options.text: When you want to introduce extensive changes to an existing theme.

options.label: C
options.text: It is not possible to customize a built-in Style Guide, since it is part of OutSystems UI.

options.label: D
options.text: When minor customizations to the base theme should be done inside the app theme.

correctText: When you want to introduce extensive changes to an existing theme.

question: Foundation may have non-business services and business services in them

options.label: A
options.text: TRUE

options.label: B
options.text: FALSE

correctText: FALSE

question: The Architecture Design Process has three steps. Which of the options below is NOT one of those steps?

options.label: A
options.text: Plan

options.label: B
options.text: Disclose

options.label: C
options.text: Assemble

options.label: D
options.text: Organize

correctText: Plan

question: What is NOT a best practice for Mobile Application Architecture: Local Storage?

options.label: A
options.text: Sync data required per use case: Sync summary data on Session start. On data selection, sync its details.Example: if you are accessing Purchasing data, sync the summary data on Session start. On selecting the Purchasing item, sync the details of the Purchasing item such as photos or price.

options.label: B
options.text: Adopt the correct sync frequency: Either at process start and online or at process/transaction end and online

options.label: C
options.text: Sync on every screen or online event

correctText: Sync on every screen or online event

question: Which of the below are FALSE about UI framework?

options.label: A
options.text: Templates - Defines the structure of the application modules. Templates contains screen definitions and basic set of functionalities, including authentication.Contains common layouts, menu, login and reference to the theme.

options.label: B
options.text: Theme - for the look and feel of your applications, which includes screen layouts, global stylesheet and grid definitions. Contains CSS styles and classes.

options.label: C
options.text: All of the above are true

options.label: D
options.text: Patterns - are blocks that provide common and reusable UI. Chat message, date picker and carousel are a few examples. Contains reusable UI components.

correctText: All of the above are true

question: What is NOT a best practice for Mobile Application Architecture: transactions & granularity?

options.label: A
options.text: Have long synchronizations in a single transaction. Better UX as app does not need to sync all the time. Is prepared for constant offline or device standby

options.label: B
options.text: Ensure order and sync granularity. Sync incrementally by entity with partial commit. This way synchronizations is prepared for constant interruptions and allow retries without repeating the entire synchronization from the start.

correctText: Have long synchronizations in a single transaction. Better UX as app does not need to sync all the time. Is prepared for constant offline or device standby

question: Which of the below matches the most to Core Module Pattern - ECS with Isolated Synchronization Logic Pattern...

options.label: A
options.text: ... a wrapper used to contain the logic, actions and data that will expose code that is inside of external library or to inspect external database and import the data structures so they can be used as entities inside of OS

options.label: B
options.text: Same as ECS with local replica but API module is provided. So any changes to the external system can notify OS, which OS then gets update from the ERP system (subscription system)

options.label: C
options.text: ... Entity is exposed as read-only and API is available to centralize business logic for entity creation/update

options.label: D
options.text: Same as Base ECS pattern, but have a local replica. Store data to serve as a local cache. Pro: Leverage Entity Use, Simpler Integration API. Con: Less impact on source system

options.label: E
options.text: ... tries to fetch data from local cache entity, if not there, get single entry from the external system. Cache only that record (read-through caching) Use when whole database too big or costly to synchronize. Integration only touches a small portion of the database. Avoid if access to lists of data is needed up front

options.label: F
options.text: ... is a pattern with two modules, a connector module that can be used to encapsulate an external API with the input/output structures and a wrapper module to expose the normalized API to the consumers.

options.label: G
options.text: ... caches only summary data that is frequently lister, joined or searched. Full detail for a single entry is fetched directly from external system. Use when whole database too big or costly to synchronize. Details are only required for single entities (not lists)

options.label: H
options.text: ... Entity is not in Outsystems but in an external ERP system. IS just makes remote call to external system/database. No data is being kept inside OS. Data retrieval may not be optimized as it needs to traverse two different systems to get the information back. Con: Integration API must support all use cases

options.label: I
options.text: ... is needed if data is coming from MULTIPLE external systems. IS will decide which driver to use depending on the data.

options.label: J
options.text: Same as ECS with local replica but synchronization logic is separated. Pro: Code independence. Consumers of CS is not affected by Sync. Sync can orchestrate several CS

correctText: Same as ECS with local replica but synchronization logic is separated. Pro: Code independence. Consumers of CS is not affected by Sync. Sync can orchestrate several CS

question: What is the Architecture Blueprint?

options.label: A
options.text: Above is the answer

options.label: B
options.text: Is the end result of Architecture Design Process. At this point you should add dependencies between the Modules.

correctText: Is the end result of Architecture Design Process. At this point you should add dependencies between the Modules.

question: Which is not a reason you should use Architecture Canvas?

options.label: A
options.text: Optimizes lifecycle independence

options.label: B

options.label: C
options.text: Enforce the reference architecture.

options.label: D

options.label: A

options.label: B
options.text: Process to package modules into apps

question: In the Architecture Canvas, where should the API module sit in?

options.label: A
options.text: Core Layer

options.label: B
options.text: End-User Layer

options.label: C
options.text: Foundation Layer

correctText: Core Layer

options.label: A

options.label: B
options.text: Apply known design patterns

options.label: C
options.text: Join conceptually-related concepts

options.text: ... Entity is not in Outsystems but in an external ERP system. IS just makes remote call to external system/database. No data is being kept inside OS. Data retrieval may not be optimized as it needs to traverse two different systems to get the information back. Con: Integration API must support all use cases

options.label: E

Cache only that record (read-through caching) Use when whole database too big or costly to synchronize. Integration only touches a small portion of the database. Avoid if access to lists of data is needed up front

options.label: J

correctText: ... Entity is exposed as read-only and API is available to centralize business logic for entity creation/update

question: Regarding Validation Rules for application composition, which of the following sentences is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: Applications in the Foundation layer can reference Applications in the End-user layer.

options.label: B
options.text: Common modules and services should be isolated in a separate app that can be referenced by other apps.

options.label: C
options.text: Owners of the Application should not be mixed, but mixing Sponsors has no direct impact on the release.

options.label: D
options.text: If you follow all the rules for validating modules, your application composition will be sound, and easy to maintain and deploy.

correctText: Common modules and services should be isolated in a separate app that can be referenced by other apps.

question: Architecture Canvas has 3 layers. Which is not part of the 3 layer framework?

options.label: A
options.text: Foundation

options.label: B
options.text: End-User Layer

options.label: C
options.text: Core

options.label: D
options.text: Ochestrator

correctText: Ochestrator

question: _Sync module is for

options.label: A
options.text: Isolated Business Logic (Actions) or Core Widgets (blocks), to manage complexity, composition or to have its own lifecycle.

options.label: B
options.text: Reusable Core Services with public entities, actions, and blocks.

options.label: C
options.text: A BL becomes a Calculation Engine if it performs complex calculations, (e.g. an invoice calculation engine or an insurance simulator). Engines are usually subject to versions.

options.label: E

correctText: Logic to Synchronize data in CS's with an external system. Isolating this logic makes the CS completely system agnostic and it’s easier to decouple or replace the external system.

question: Elements such as the menu and login can be shared across different apps. In a specific use case, both the menu and login are defined inside the theme. Which use case is this?

options.text: Intranet

correctText: Core Widgets (blocks), to manage complexity, composition or to have its own lifecycle.

question: ISO/IEC 25010:2011 adds two more aspect to ISO/IEC 9126:1991. Which of the below is not part of it?

options.label: A

options.label: B
options.text: Security : Security ensure software solutions can protect information and data

options.label: C
options.text: Integration : Integration to allow data exchange with other software

correctText: Integration : Integration to allow data exchange with other software

question: The Architecture Canvas is a multi-layer framework. Which of the following is NOT a benefit of this framework?

options.label: C
options.text: It optimizes lifecycle independence.

question: There are 3 common scenarios for Sharing a Style Guide. Which of the below is not part of the scenario.

options.label: A
options.text: Independent Apps : Own Menu, Own Login Flow. Login and Menu is defined in the Custom Template. Applications reference to its own Application Theme thus do not use the Login and Menu in the Custom Template.

options.label: B
options.text: Intranet (Single Sign On): Own Menu, Common Login Flow. Menu is defined in the Custom Template, but Login is defined in the Custom Theme. Application reference the Custom Them which picks up the Login.

options.label: C
options.text: Portal : Common Menu, Common Login Flow. Login and Menu is defined in the Custom Theme. Application Theme reference the Custom Theme thus have a shared menu and login flow.

options.label: D
options.text: Enterprise Apps: Common Menu, Common Login Flow. Menu is defined in the Custom Theme, but Login is defined in the Custom Template.

correctText: Enterprise Apps: Common Menu, Common Login Flow. Menu is defined in the Custom Theme, but Login is defined in the Custom Template.

question: Which of the below is NOT a disadvantage of having a fragmented system or microservices?

options.label: A
options.text: Complicated Debugging and troubleshooting : root cause may be deep inside the chain of services

options.label: B
options.text: Fault tolerance : communication errors, service consistency

options.label: C
options.text: Inter-process communication : network latency and hiccups

options.label: D
options.text: Monitoring & Logging : for effective monitoring & logging, requires centralized service

options.label: E
options.text: Limited data mashup in memory and limited to APIs

options.label: F
options.text: Multiple transactions committed independently

options.label: G
options.text: All of the above

options.label: H
options.text: Security : need to manage credentials and access management

correctText: All of the above

options.label: A

options.label: B
options.text: Same as Base ECS pattern, but have a local replica. Store data to serve as a local cache. Pro: Leverage Entity Use, Simpler Integration API. Con: Less impact on source system

options.label: C
options.text: ... Entity is exposed as read-only and API is available to centralize business logic for entity creation/update

options.label: D

Cache only that record (read-through caching) Use when whole database too big or costly to synchronize. Integration only touches a small portion of the database. Avoid if access to lists of data is needed up front

options.label: E
options.text: ... is needed if data is coming from MULTIPLE external systems. IS will decide which driver to use depending on the data.

options.label: F
options.text: ... Entity is not in Outsystems but in an external ERP system. IS just makes remote call to external system/database. No data is being kept inside OS. Data retrieval may not be optimized as it needs to traverse two different systems to get the information back. Con: Integration API must support all use cases

options.text: ... caches only summary data that is frequently lister, joined or searched. Full detail for a single entry is fetched directly from external system. Use when whole database too big or costly to synchronize. Details are only required for single entities (not lists)

correctText: Same as Base ECS pattern, but have a local replica. Store data to serve as a local cache. Pro: Leverage Entity Use, Simpler Integration API. Con: Less impact on source system

question: Which of the following options denotes the advantages of defining a Style Guide up front?

options.label: A
options.text: Speed up the development phase.

options.label: B
options.text: Improve performance and maintainability.

options.label: C
options.text: Security and scalability.

options.label: D
options.text: Allows apps and the Style Guide to be deployed to Production.

correctText: Improve performance and maintainability.

question: There are generally 4 common style guide scenario. Which of the below is NOT a common style guide scenario?

options.label: A
options.text: Modify Built-in Style Guide: Use when only small changes are required for single application. First, create a custom theme that REFERENCE the Base Theme. Second, clone a Custom Template from the Base Template but this custom theme will reference the custom theme created previously. Third, reference that application to the custom Theme and Template.

options.label: B
options.text: Build Your Own: Use when custom style guide cannot benefit from any existing theme. Just like Clone a Built In scenario, clone both Custom Theme and Custom Template from the Base Theme and Base Template, however clone the Base Theme from Outsystems UI.Since the theme is very basic, front-end developer will have to expand and design the theme from there.

options.label: C
options.text: Clone a Built-In: Use when introducing extensive changes to existing theme. First, CLONE a custom theme from a Base Theme. Second, clone a Custom Template from the Base Template but this custom theme will reference the custom theme created previously. Third, create new applications based on the Custom Theme and Custom Template.Base Theme can be Outsystems UI or your own custom theme.

options.label: D
options.text: Specialize a Built-in: Use when introducing changes to an existing theme for multiple apps. First, create a custom theme that REFERENCE the Base Theme. Second, clone a Custom Template from the Base Template but this custom theme will reference the custom theme created previously. Third, create new applications based on the Custom Theme and Custom Template

options.label: E
options.text: Built-in Style Guide: Uses Built-in style guide from Outsystems UI. Minor customizations to the Base Theme should be done inside the App Theme.

correctText: Modify Built-in Style Guide: Use when only small changes are required for single application. First, create a custom theme that REFERENCE the Base Theme. Second, clone a Custom Template from the Base Template but this custom theme will reference the custom theme created previously. Third, reference that application to the custom Theme and Template.

options.text: framework to support application architecture design in Outsystems

options.label: B
options.text: framework to support Architecture Design Process in Outsystems

options.label: C
options.text: framework to support Architecture Conventions for Modules in Outsystems

correctText: framework to support application architecture design in Outsystems

question: Which of the below is not part of the Architecture Validation

options.label: A
options.text: No Downward References To Foundation Modules

options.label: B
options.text: No Side References Between End-User Modules

options.label: C
options.text: No Upward References

options.label: D
options.text: No Circular References

correctText: No Downward References To Foundation Modules

question: Which of the following is an Assembling principle?

options.label: A
options.text: Disclose business concepts and integration needs.

options.label: B
options.text: Keep concepts with different lifecycles apart.

options.label: C
options.text: Isolate all business concepts.

options.label: D
options.text: Join all integrations into a single Module.

correctText: Keep concepts with different lifecycles apart.

correctText: Isolated Business Logic (Actions), to manage complexity, composition or to have its own lifecycle.

question: In which Architecture Canvas layer do you expect to have a higher reusability rate?

options.label: A
options.text: Core layer

options.label: B
options.text: Foundation layer

options.label: C
options.text: End-User layer

correctText: Foundation layer

question: Considering Architecture Dashboard(AI Mentor), which of the following sentences is FALSE?

options.label: A
options.text: Architecture Dashboard performs a code analysis that uncovers patterns related to performance, security, architecture and maintainability.

options.label: B
options.text: Architecture Dashboard allows you to automatically apply a solution to solve a found code pattern.

correctText: Architecture Dashboard allows you to automatically apply a solution to solve a found code pattern.

question: Which of the below is NOT a Synchronization Best Practice for mobile application?

options.label: A
options.text: Only synchronize delta : Use control timestamps

options.label: B
options.text: Ensure sync granularity: Prepare for constant retries without repeating work due to network loss. Sync in chunks, incremental by entity

options.label: C
options.text: Sync only when required. At session start: sync summary data to local storageAt process start: sync process detail to local storageAt process/transaction end, sync back to server

options.label: D
options.text: Avoid long synchronization in a single transaction

options.label: E
options.text: Do not use Entity Bulk Operation to ensure small lightweight updates

options.label: F
options.text: Only synchronize relevant data

options.label: G
options.text: Sync only when strictly required

options.label: H
options.text: Don't sync on every screen or online event

correctText: Do not use Entity Bulk Operation to ensure small lightweight updates

question: Of the options below, which one is a benefit of adopting the Architecture Canvas?

options.label: A
options.text: It's a faster architecture design

options.label: B
options.text: It promotes the business users' collaboration and understanding

options.label: C
options.text: It's an automatic way to find and fix architecture issues.

options.label: D
options.text: It's a systematic approach to architecture design

correctText: It's a systematic approach to architecture design

question: Which of the below is NOT a reason why application Architecture is important when building quality software solutions?

options.label: A
options.text: Supports Planning : Helps the team anticipate and build solutions

options.label: B
options.text: Reduces Risk : Architecture is a cost effective way to mitigate substantial risks and ensure success

options.label: C
options.text: Facilitate Change : When something comes up that requires you to veer from the original design or path, architecture of your system can show you exactly what needs to change and help with communication and planning.

options.label: D
options.text: Drives Consensus : Helps create common ground with the team

options.label: E
options.text: Helps Communication : Helps you to get buy in from the business and to communicate overall plan to them

options.label: F
options.text: Manages Complexity : Helps reduce emerging complexity by focusing on relevant properties and omitting irrelevant details, thus leading to simpler representation

options.label: G
options.text: Reduces Costs : Architecture benefits are not only technical, but affect how you operate

correctText: Helps Communication : Helps you to get buy in from the business and to communicate overall plan to them

question: SSL Pinning is a security best practice for mobile application. Which of the below is FALSE about SSL Pinning?

options.label: A
options.text: Works on the client side and adds verification of the server certificate against hashes of public keys, which are pre-bundled with the mobile app

options.label: B
options.text: SSL Pinning or HTTP Public Key Pinning (HPKP) helps reduce man-in-the-middle attacks

options.label: C
options.text: SSL Pinning does not mean hardcoding the public key into the code.

options.label: D
options.text: All of the above are true

correctText: SSL Pinning does not mean hardcoding the public key into the code.

question: Which of the below is NOT a suitable advice for designing a Parallel mobile local storage?

options.label: A
options.text: Use Fetch Data : Avoid cascading aggregates in OnInitialize and OnReady

options.label: B
options.text: Allow table dependency : Normalize tables to promote parallel data fetch

options.label: C
options.text: Avoid generic tables : Contains too much data, not all are relevant

correctText: Allow table dependency : Normalize tables to promote parallel data fetch

question: Considering Discovery, which of the following sentences is FALSE?

options.label: A
options.text: Discovery allows you to determine for every module, which elements are being consumed by other modules.

options.label: B
options.text: Discovery cannot categorise modules to domains

options.label: C
options.text: Discovery only validates architecture rules at the module level.

options.label: D
options.text: Discovery automatically assigns a module to an Architecture Canvas layer, following the module's naming convention.

correctText: Discovery only validates architecture rules at the module level.

question: What is a best practice for Mobile Application Architecture: Local Storage?

options.label: A
options.text: Have a local storage inside the CS Module instead of M_CS Module

options.label: B
options.text: Map server side static entities into local entities

options.label: C
options.text: Use a centralized local storage for all mobile applications to ensure maintainability

options.label: D
options.text: Do not use local storage(mobile device), instead try to use central storage(server side)

correctText: Map server side static entities into local entities

question: ISO/IEC 9126:1991 included several different dimensions. Which is NOT part of ISO/IEC 9126:1991?

options.label: A
options.text: Security : Security which relates to the strength of the of the software against cybersecurity attacks

options.label: B
options.text: Efficiency : Efficiency which relates to the performance to the amount of resources used

options.label: C
options.text: Reliability : Reliability which ensures that a software solution can maintain it performance over a period of time

options.label: D
options.text: Usability : Usability from the point of view of the users

options.label: E
options.text: Functionality : Functionality as a set of functions that satisfy needs

options.label: F
options.text: Maintainability : Maintainability which relates to the effort needed to make modifications

options.label: G
options.text: Portability : Portability which defines the ability to move software between environments

correctText: Security : Security which relates to the strength of the of the software against cybersecurity attacks

question: Consider the common style guide scenarios presented in this course. Which of the following statements is true?

options.label: A
options.text: The "clone a built-in Style Guide" should be used when you want to extend an existing theme.

options.label: B
options.text: The "specialize a built-in Style Guide" scenario should be used when the changes to the base theme are not extensive.

options.label: C
options.text: Build-your-own Style Guide should be used when the changes in the theme are not extensive.

correctText: The "specialize a built-in Style Guide" scenario should be used when the changes to the base theme are not extensive.

question: Which of the below is NOT part of the three step process of Architecture Design Process?

options.label: A
options.text: Organize

options.label: B
options.text: Disclose

options.label: C
options.text: Shape

options.label: D
options.text: Assemble

correctText: Shape

question: In OutSystems, a Core Application can contain ...

options.label: A
options.text: End-User, Core, and Foundation Modules.

options.label: B
options.text: Foundation and Core Modules

options.label: C
options.text: Only Foundation Modules.

options.label: D
options.text: End-User and Core Modules.

correctText: Foundation and Core Modules

question: "Spaghetti Architecture" is also known as 'tightly coupled architecture' or 'brittle architecture'. Which is NOT a reason why is "Spaghetti Architecture" bad?

options.label: A
options.text: Hinder future changes as they become less flexible and difficult to manage

options.label: B
options.text: Any changes is maintainable and easy to deploy

options.label: C
options.text: One small change in a component cause a series of cascading effects on other components

correctText: Any changes is maintainable and easy to deploy

question: Which of the following is NOT a benefit of having well-defined application architecture?

options.label: A
options.text: Reduces risk

options.label: B
options.text: Reduces costs

options.label: C
options.text: Supports planning

options.label: D
options.text: Poor service abstraction

correctText: Poor service abstraction

question: Consider the following Module names and the suggested naming conventions for Modules. Which of these is a mobile Module?

options.label: A
options.text: Mobile_API

options.label: B
options.text: Customer_CW

options.label: C
options.text: Pricing_MBL

options.label: D
options.text: Service_CS

correctText: Pricing_MBL
